title: The block browser
text: Adding single-shot works two ways: Click card body add block immediately sensible defaults - freshly-generated unique id, block’s default title, (append / prepend) default link input port. Open card chevron tweak id, title, link id input port first, click -card add button. Either way browser publishes single Shiny input <mode>_block_commit carrying block create.

title: The block browser
code: library(shiny) library(blockr.ui) library(blockr.core)  board <- new_board(blocks = list(src = new_dataset_block()))  ui <- fluidPage(   actionButton("open", "Add block"),   sidebar_ui("panel", side = "right", width = "420px"),   verbatimTextOutput("spec") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   observeEvent(input$open, {     show_sidebar(       "panel",       title = "Add new block",       ui = block_browser_ui(session$ns, board, mode = "add")     )   })    observeEvent(input$add_block_commit, {     str(input$add_block_commit)   })    output$spec <- renderPrint({     spec <- input$add_block_commit     if (is.null(spec)) cat("(no commit yet)") else str(spec)   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: The block browser
text: <mode>_block_commit input single block create: Fields applicable current mode NULL (R reads JS’s null NULL). action handler turns single update() call board.
code: list(   type = "dataset_block",   id = "intimate_hind",   # unique, avoids the board's existing ids   title = NULL,           # NULL -> the block keeps its default title   link_id = NULL,         # set for append / prepend   block_input = NULL,     # set for append (the new block's input port)   target_input = NULL     # set for prepend into a multi-input target )

title: The block browser
text: suggested id (link_id) defaults generated avoiding board’s existing block / link ids. action handler re-renders browser updated board add (e.g. sidebar pinned), next suggestion fresh. means clicking card several times produces several distinct blocks - simple way , say, drop three dataset blocks onto board.

title: The block browser
text: per-card form shows fields relevant mode: prepend, target_input shown target block one input slot (e.g. merge block, arity 2); arity-1 variadic targets hide .

title: The block browser
text: search bar case-insensitive substring match card’s name + description + package + category. Empty category sections collapse automatically. (future iteration may replace AI-assisted search maps natural-language question candidate blocks - see change’s design notes.)

title: Driving the sidebar primitive
text: inputs inside body (input$block_name, input$block_kind, input$confirm) live calling session - exactly inside modalDialog(). nested moduleServer() required.
code: library(shiny) library(blockr.ui)  ui <- fluidPage(   actionButton("open", "Open sidebar"),   sidebar_ui("main_sidebar") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   observeEvent(input$open, {     show_sidebar(       "main_sidebar",       title = "Add a new block",       ui = tagList(         textInput("block_name", "Name"),         selectInput("block_kind", "Type", c("data", "transform", "plot")),         actionButton("confirm", "Add", class = "btn-primary")       )     )   })    observeEvent(input$confirm, {     # validate, mutate state, ...     hide_sidebar("main_sidebar")   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Driving the sidebar primitive
text: sidebar_ui() call also registers Shiny input id, exposing two logical fields: input[[id]]$open - TRUE panel open, FALSE user closes (Esc, X button) server calls hide_sidebar(). input[[id]]$pinned - TRUE user panel pinned.
code: observe({   state <- input$main_sidebar   req(state)   cat("open:", state$open, " pinned:", state$pinned, "\n") })

title: Driving the sidebar primitive
text: panel whose body known UI-build time (settings form, help panel, “” sheet), don’t need server round-trip open . Pre-render body sidebar_ui() time put data-blockr-sidebar-target = "<id>" element toggle . Clicking gear toggles panel open / closed entirely client. inputs inside (input$opt_a, input$opt_b) observable server exactly sat page directly. later show_sidebar("settings_sidebar") (ui) still opens panel server needed; show_sidebar("settings_sidebar", ui = <tags>) replaces pre-rendered body want swap dynamically.
code: ui <- fluidPage(   actionButton(     "settings_btn", icon("gear"),     `data-blockr-sidebar-target` = "settings_sidebar"   ),   sidebar_ui(     "settings_sidebar",     side = "left",     title = "Board options",     ui = tagList(       checkboxInput("opt_a", "Option A", value = TRUE),       sliderInput("opt_b", "Option B", min = 0, max = 100, value = 50)     )   ) )

title: Driving the sidebar primitive
text: show_sidebar() replaces panel’s body title place, pin state bound panel element. Re-showing panel fresh content caller (“chain confirm” flow, keep_or_hide_sidebar() builds ) supported pattern. Two unrelated concerns - say “Settings” panel “Add block” form - mount two sidebar_ui() panels distinct ids. Sharing one id unrelated callers means second show_sidebar() silently overwrite first, including pinned content user expected stay put.

title: Driving the sidebar primitive
text: common renderer pattern open sidebar “first block” hint session starts empty board, still respecting later user dismissal. recipe built top show_sidebar() input[[id]]$open - special opt-needed sidebar_ui(). Push-mode panels reflow page content via padding <html>, driven --blockr-sidebar-width-left --blockr-sidebar-width-right custom properties (nonzero sides open). legacy --blockr-sidebar-width also set open side’s width single panel open, existing consumers form margin-right: var(--blockr-sidebar-width, 0px) keep working.
code: empty_board_renderer <- function(id, board) {   moduleServer(id, function(input, output, session) {     auto_opened <- reactiveVal(FALSE)      observe({       if (auto_opened()) return()       if (length(blockr.core::board_blocks(board)) > 0L) return()        show_sidebar(         session$ns("main_sidebar"),         title = "Add your first block",         ui = tagList(           p("Pick a block to get started."),           actionButton(session$ns("add_dataset"), "Dataset block"),           actionButton(session$ns("add_import"), "Import data")         )       )       auto_opened(TRUE)     })      # Once the user dismisses the hint, input[[id]]$open flips to FALSE     # and the auto_opened flag prevents this observer from re-opening.   }) }

title: Authors and Citation
text: David Granjon. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Granjon D (2026). blockr.ui: User Interface Components 'blockr' Ecosystem. R package version 0.0.0.9000, https://bristolmyerssquibb.github.io/blockr.ui/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {blockr.ui: User Interface Components for the 'blockr' Ecosystem},   author = {David Granjon},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {https://bristolmyerssquibb.github.io/blockr.ui/}, }

what: User Interface Components for the blockr Ecosystem
title: User Interface Components for the blockr Ecosystem
text: User-interface primitives shared across blockr ecosystem. Designed sit blockr.core renderers blockr.dock.

title: User Interface Components for the blockr Ecosystem
text: can install development version blockr.ui GitHub :

title: User Interface Components for the blockr Ecosystem
text: slide-panel hosts arbitrary Shiny content. See vignette("sidebar", package = "blockr.ui") full API. blockr.ui minimal sidebar example
code: library(shiny) library(blockr.ui)  ui <- fluidPage(   titlePanel("blockr.ui sidebar primitive"),   actionButton("open", "Open sidebar", class = "btn-primary"),   verbatimTextOutput("state"),   sidebar_ui("main_sidebar") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   observeEvent(input$open, {     show_sidebar(       "main_sidebar",       title = "Add a new block",       ui = tagList(         textInput("block_name", "Name"),         selectInput(           "block_kind",           "Type",           c("data", "transform", "plot")         ),         actionButton("confirm", "Add", class = "btn-primary")       )     )   })    observeEvent(input$confirm, {     hide_sidebar("main_sidebar")   })    output$state <- renderPrint({     state <- input$main_sidebar     if (is.null(state)) return("sidebar: not yet rendered")     sprintf(       "sidebar: open = %s, pinned = %s",       state$open, state$pinned     )   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: User Interface Components for the blockr Ecosystem
text: Please note blockr.ui project released Contributor Code Conduct. contributing project, agree abide terms.

title: Block browser — block-browser
text: card-list block picker designed live inside sidebar primitive. Replaces legacy single-select form used add / append / prepend block flows blockr.dock. card represents registered block (one row blockr.core::available_blocks()), grouped category filterable search bar.

title: Block browser — block-browser
code: block_browser_ui(id, board, target = NULL)  append_to(block_id)  prepend_to(block_id)  block_browser_server(id)  block_browser_dep()

title: Block browser — block-browser
text: id Module id. usual Shiny modules, pass NS(id)("...") parent *_ui() call site bare id *_server(). board current board state. Used resolve target block's input arity (prepend) seed unique default ids avoiding board's existing block / link ids. target new block attaches. NULL (default) plain add; append_to() appends source block; prepend_to() prepends target block. block_id Block id new block attaches : source block append_to(), target block prepend_to().

title: Block browser — block-browser
text: block_browser_ui() returns htmltools::tag block_browser_dep() attached. block_browser_server() returns shiny::reactive committed block spec (list), firing per add. append_to() / prepend_to() return bb_target descriptor. block_browser_dep() returns htmltools::htmlDependency.

title: Block browser — block-browser
text: Adding block single-shot works two ways: Click card body add block immediately sensible defaults (freshly-generated unique id, block's default title, - append / prepend - default link port). Open card via chevron tweak id, title, link id input port first, click -card add button. block browser Shiny module. block_browser_ui(id, ...) renders panel; block_browser_server(id) returns reactive fires per add block create: list(type, id, title, link_id, block_input, target_input). Fields applicable current flow NULL. suggested ids generated avoiding board's existing block / link ids, clicking card repeatedly (pinned sidebar) yields distinct blocks. flow selected target argument: NULL (default) adds standalone block, append_to() appends source block, prepend_to() prepends target block. append / prepend constructors always carry block attach , flow trigger can never disagree. Internally panel publishes value blockr.ui.blockBrowser Shiny.InputBinding (root element's id NS(id)("commit")); value carries internal nonce repeat adds re-fire events. block_browser_server() strips nonce hands back clean spec, consumers never see .

title: Block browser — block-browser
code: if (interactive()) {   shiny::shinyApp(     ui = shiny::fluidPage(       sidebar_ui("panel", side = "right"),       shiny::actionButton("open", "Open block browser")     ),     server = function(input, output, session) {       added <- block_browser_server("browser")       shiny::observeEvent(input$open, {         show_sidebar(           "panel",           title = "Add new block",           ui = block_browser_ui(session$ns("browser"), NULL)         )       })       shiny::observeEvent(added(), print(added()))     }   ) }

what: blockr.ui: User Interface Components for the 'blockr' Ecosystem — blockr.ui-package
title: blockr.ui: User Interface Components for the 'blockr' Ecosystem — blockr.ui-package
text: User-interface primitives shared across 'blockr' ecosystem. Designed sit 'blockr.core' renderers 'blockr.dock'.

title: blockr.ui: User Interface Components for the 'blockr' Ecosystem — blockr.ui-package
text: Maintainer: David Granjon david@cynkra.com

title: Sidebar primitive — sidebar
text: Bootstrap-free slide-panel mirroring shiny::showModal() / shiny::removeModal() spirit. sidebar_ui() mounts empty panel per page; show_sidebar() hide_sidebar() drive content moduleServer() body, exactly like modal.

title: Sidebar primitive — sidebar
code: sidebar_ui(   id,   ui = NULL,   title = NULL,   side = c("right", "left"),   width = "360px",   mode = c("overlay", "push") )  sidebar_dep()  show_sidebar(   id,   ui = NULL,   title = NULL,   session = shiny::getDefaultReactiveDomain() )  hide_sidebar(id, session = shiny::getDefaultReactiveDomain())  sidebar_state(id, session = shiny::getDefaultReactiveDomain())  keep_or_hide_sidebar(   id,   ui,   title = NULL,   session = shiny::getDefaultReactiveDomain() )

title: Sidebar primitive — sidebar
text: id character scalar. DOM id used panel element. id show_sidebar() / hide_sidebar() target R reads input[[id]] observe {open, pinned} state. ui Shiny tag tree render inside panel body. Pre-rendered via htmltools::renderTags() dependencies travel content. title Optional title shown panel header. NULL clears . side side viewport panel slides . width CSS length applied panel --blockr-sidebar-panel-width. CSS bundle uses variable panel's width. mode One "overlay" (default) "push". "overlay" mode panel hovers page rest layout untouched. "push" mode bundled CSS adds padding-<side>: var(--blockr-sidebar-width) <body> whenever panel open, page content shifts aside instead covered. choice independent pin state. session Shiny session. Defaults calling reactive domain - shiny::showModal().

title: Sidebar primitive — sidebar
text: sidebar_ui() returns htmltools::tag sidebar_dep() attached. sidebar_dep() returns htmltools::htmlDependency. show_sidebar() hide_sidebar() called side effect (custom message client) return invisible(NULL). sidebar_state() returns list list(open, pinned) logicals, read root session's input. Useful action handler wants decide whether chain (re-render form) close panel successful confirm: pinned panels stay open per user's opt-, unpinned panels close. keep_or_hide_sidebar() convenience built top sidebar_state() action-handler confirm flow: panel pinned re-shows supplied (fresh) ui user can chain another action; otherwise hides panel.

title: Sidebar primitive — sidebar
text: panel id single concern: show_sidebar() replaces panel's body title place, pin state bound panel element. Re-showing fresh content caller supported pattern (see keep_or_hide_sidebar()). Two unrelated concerns mount sidebar_ui(id) - reusing one id different callers silently overwrite pinned panel's content next time show_sidebar() called . panel can opened two complementary ways. original "modal-like" form show_sidebar(id, ui = <tags>) server, rendered demand. "offcanvas-like" form pre-renders body sidebar_ui() time (pass ui optionally title) opens client-side: element page carrying data-blockr-sidebar-target = "<id>" toggles matching panel click, R round-trip. right model static settings panel triggered navbar icon - click latency stays one frame instead full Shiny round-trip. two forms coexist DOM mount: later show_sidebar(id, ui = ...) replaces pre-rendered body consumer later wants dynamic behaviour, show_sidebar(id) (ui) opens pre-rendered panel server without re-shipping HTML.

title: Sidebar primitive — sidebar
code: if (interactive()) {   library(shiny)   ui <- fluidPage(     actionButton("open", "Open sidebar"),     sidebar_ui("panel", side = "right")   )   server <- function(input, output, session) {     observeEvent(input$open, {       show_sidebar(         "panel",         title = "Hello",         ui = tagList(           textInput("name", "Name"),           actionButton("ok", "OK")         )       )     })     observeEvent(input$ok, hide_sidebar("panel"))   }   shinyApp(ui, server) }
